prevalence.value: 5-25% (older adults; higher with diabetes and frailty)
prevalence.trend: increasing with aging and diabetes burden

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Orthostatic BP drop criteria; evaluate medications and volume status

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions of older adults at risk
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; depends on medication review and hydration

regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Hydration, medication adjustment, compression, treat anemia; specialist therapy for refractory cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High comorbidity burden and limited geriatric review services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Simple low-cost measures can reduce symptoms when implemented
